name: Queers and Beers (and Other Delightful Drinks!)
description:
Queers and Beers (and Other Delightful Drinks!)

Calling all people of the rainbow! Want to learn more about our hacker/maker space while meeting new people? Come to Queers and Beers (and Other Delightful Drinks)!

This meetup is open to all queer/LGBTQIA2S+ people whether you've been to Unallocated before or not.

The tone will be casual: come as you are and be prepared to socialize. Bring drinks and snacks for yourself or to share. There will be some craft supplies if you want to make something.

THINGS TO BRING: Yourself, some snacks, and a tasty beverage (please be responsible and 21+ if you plan to bring alcohol)

PRESENTER: Thea

name: Web3 Maryland Blockchain Mixer
description:
Maryland, let’s connect through blockchain! 🧩🚀

Join Web3 Maryland and Stand With Crypto to support community & connect the network of founders, developers, and dreamers building in the Old Line State. Whether you're launching a protocol, writing your first smart contract, or exploring the space, you’ve got a home here.

Hosted by:🌐 Web3 Maryland 

Powered by:🛡️ Stand With Crypto 🛠️ Maryland Blockchain Association🏛️ Web3DC**Food will be provided**

Come build with us.Come learn.Come connect.

🌐 Web3 is happening in Maryland — don’t miss it.

name: Baltimore Climate Tech Meetup June 2025
description:
Join the Baltimore Climate Tech community for our June networking meetup at Union Brewery. Whether you're a climate veteran or looking to transition, join us to meet new friends and colleagues to chat about sustainability and climate tech.

​These happy hours often include a few dozen of us working in Climate Tech, including founders, funders, builders, and people looking to learn more about transitioning into Climate Tech in and around the Baltimore area.

​Let's learn from, support, and inspire each other around technology aimed at combating our climate crisis.

​Please share our events with friends and colleagues as we grow the Baltimore Climate Tech community with your help!

calendar.name: Baltimore Climate Tech Meetups
calendar.description: We host monthly gatherings for anyone interested, working, investing, and buliding in climate tech around Baltimore.

Co-founder, HBCU Environmental Justice Technical Collaborative
Founder, The Butterfly Effect

featured_guests.name: Mike Ducker
featured_guests.bio: 20 years of startup ecosystem building - focused on helping to build the Maryland Startup Ecosystem at MEIA

featured_guests.name: Kristen Shaw
featured_guests.bio:
Kristen Shaw is a seasoned growth marketing strategist with two decades of experience driving results for tech companies and startups. She combines strategic vision, data-driven analysis, and adaptability to deliver impactful growth. 

Kristen's expertise spans demand generation, content marketing, and building high-performing teams, while optimizing processes for efficiency and scalability.

Currently serving as the National Marketing Chair for Cleantech Open and Head of Growth for Eristem. 
Kristen is focusing her skills on climate tech and sustainability initiatives. She brings a unique perspective to businesses looking to make an impact while driving growth, calmly navigating the rapid changes and ambiguity inherent in the evolving climate sectors.

registration_questions.question: How has the Baltimore Climate Tech Meetup benefited you? Catalytic connections, peer support, new knowledge, or more? Please share a story of the value our group has given you.
Please share your phone number and we will add you to our WhatsApp group.

name: Tony Robbins Enthusiasts
description: This online meetup is for anyone who are followers of Tony Robbins teachings and would like to make connections. Either you have read Tony Robbins books, listened to his audiobooks, went to his seminars, did his courses or listened to his podcasts. This is a great place to make connections, find support, and share knowledge. It will be a relaxed informal meeting networking and learning from each other.

name: Casual Coding, Chat, & Coffee
description:
Join us for an informal gathering of Python enthusiasts at ZiPani, where we'll blend our love for coding with the rich aroma of coffee! Whether you're a seasoned developer or just starting out, this meetup is designed for everyone who enjoys Python programming in a relaxed environment.

Plan:

* **Casual Coding:** Work on your personal projects, get help, or share your code with others.
* **Open Discussion:** From Python libraries to best practices, let's talk about what's on your mind.
* **Networking:** Meet other local Python developers, exchange ideas, and maybe find your next coding buddy or collaborator.

Why Attend?

* Learn something new or share your knowledge in a friendly atmosphere.
* Get real-time help with coding problems or brainstorm new ideas.
* Discover local job opportunities or side projects within the Python community.

No need for formal presentations or slides; this is all about conversation and collaboration. Feel free to come and go as your schedule allows.

name: All Levels: Online Ask Me Anything WordPress!
description:
Ask Us Anything WordPress!

Register for it by filling out this form: https://forms.gle/Th6AZUbS2GoiCEJz5

This is a virtual meetup on Zoom for you to ask your WordPress questions and help you fix problems with your website.

The answerer for this meetup will be Gen Herres, owner of Anphira Web Design & Development, and a WordPress veteran of over a decade. She's built and remodeled hundreds of WordPress sites.

Don't have a question? You can still come and see what others ask - you might learn something!

We're asking you to submit questions in advance (please use the form at https://forms.gle/Th6AZUbS2GoiCEJz5) so that we can gather common ones together to answer them. But if you'd like to share your screen when it's your turn during the meetup, just let us know on the registration form!

You'll be muted on entry, we'll be using the chat to queue people up/ask follow-on questions.

The meetup will run 11am ET until 12pm ET or until we finish up the questions, whichever comes first. If you pre-submitted your question and are not present, then your question will be skipped.

Meetup is scheduled to be recorded. Recordings are posted about 2 weeks after the meetup and are provided courtesy of our sponsor, [Obee Digital Consulting](https://obeedigitalconsulting.com/).

How to get notified about the recording: either subscribe to the [youtube channel](https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLoT0wv53Bj-BGfD8fPhMRC3qTtPMZHioI), RSVP "yes" to the meetup, or [join the mailing list](https://share.mailercloud.com/form?id=a0NzdUAxMTI1OUAwMDAwMA==).

name: Ellicott City Crypto x Tech Unity Cookout
description:
Join us for an unforgettable afternoon blending tech, and backyard vibes! Ellicott City Crytpo is proud to support the Tech Unity Cookout hosted by Code Collective.

Whether you’re deep into Web3 or just tech-curious, this event brings together builders, investors, creatives, and curious minds from across the Baltimore tech scene.

Got interesting ideas you want to share? This is the perfect space to connect with a diverse crowd of tech-savvy innovators.
Expect real conversations about building together—locally, inclusively, and with purpose. Come for the connections, stay for the food, music, and good vibes.

Participating groups

* **Code Collective**
* **Baltimore Node Makerspace**
* **Baltimore Tech**
* **Brady UX**
* **DevOps Columbia**
* **Blk Tech Connect**
* **Baltimore Indie Game Devs**
*

https://www.meetup.com/code-collective/events/307426667/?eventOrigin=group_events_list

name: [IN-PERSON/ONLINE] Game Dev Meetup (Elkridge Branch!)
description:
**IN-PERSON + ONLINE**

*In-person is at the **Elkridge Branch Library** in the* *Grist Mill Room.*

Join us **IN-PERSON** or ONLINE. Anyone with an interest in game design and development is welcome, from beginners to veteran professionals.

We will be meeting in the Grist Mill Room at the Elkridge Branch Library (6540 Washington Blvd, Elkridge, MD 21075). (Not an official Howard County Library System event.)

If you want to attend virtually, the Discord server link is provided in the main monthly (1st of the month) meetup details. An organizer will ensure that a laptop is provided on-site to connect the Discord attendees with those physically on-site. It takes us a few minutes to set up the projector, so hold tight when you join the Discord voice chat channel. We'll be there soon!

name: [ONLINE] Game Dev Meetup (Monthly Meetup)
description:
ONLINE MEETUP USING DISCORD. (Discord link visible after RSVPing.)

We're meeting on Discord! The link to join the Baltimore Indie Game Devs discord server will be in your RSVP message.

Join us to discuss game development, talk shop, and bounce ideas. Anyone with an interest in game design and development is welcome, from beginners to veteran professionals.

If you're working on a game, you're welcome to show it and ask for feedback. There will be plenty of time to chat and talk shop, too.

name: [IN-PERSON] Summer Game Dev Picnic
description:
Come join us for the Baltimore Indie Game Devs potluck picnic at BIG member Allen Lambert's house in Marriottsville just west of Baltimore. This picnic is for all local game devs in the Baltimore-Washington area. Allen will be manning the grill, and there will be outdoor games as well as tabletop games to play.

If you'd like to bring something to the potluck, please add it in the Comments section.

Notes:

* This is a NO ALCOHOL event.
* In the event of rain, we may postpone this event.
* **We'll send the street address to RSVP'd attendees one week before the picnic and the day before the picnic. Please send a message to Allen Lambert or Tony Li if you'd like the address sooner.**

name: Zero to Launch: Fueling Your Mission with AI
description:
This month we are goin to talk AI with Jeffrey Scruggs. Zero to Launch: Fueling Your Mission wit AI

Jeffrey Scruggs is a software engineer, entrepreneur, and creative technologist with a background in civil and transportation engineering. After teaching himself Python and AI while working full-time, he transitioned into tech roles with the Naval Surface Warfare Center and Booz Allen Hamilton. He now serves as Chief Technology Officer at Venture for T.H.E.M., supporting Black and Brown entrepreneurs, and is also the founder of Majestic Light Group, a software consulting firm. Jeffrey blends technical expertise with a deep commitment to community, using creativity and innovation to drive social impact and inspire the next generation. His journey is rooted in faith, purpose, and the power of meaningful connections.

**Keep convos going on Slack - [Join Baltimore Tech Meetup](https://join.slack.com/t/baltimoretech/shared_invite/zt-34ke7zm93-p5~oAmjtCHDqhxhw1BBU2g) or follow us [LinkedIn](https://www.linkedin.com/company/baltimore-tech-meetup/) or [Instagram](https://www.instagram.com/baltimoretechmeetup/)**

**Date: June 26h 2026**
**Location: Spark Baltimore - 8 Market Pl Suite 300, Baltimore, MD 21202**
**Time: 5:30pm to 7:00 pm**

\#\#\# A few logistical details:

* Spark Baltimore is located in PowerPlant Live! next to Charm City Clue Room on the first floor.
* We will meet for drinks in the area after.
* By attending, all participants commit to upholding and being subject to our[ ](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1ihtT6eeIuc6KXqQX4KbWpKTY3PLJ6RjgoTqXDY1QPgw/edit?usp=sharing)[Code of Conduct](https://docs.google.com/document/d/1ctYtmK0_q16rm7Vk3hdBkC6o_4MQZ_148bEKLR9JoCE/edit?usp=sharing).
* By attending, participants also acknowledge and grant permission for the use of photographs they may appear in for promotional material by the organizers or sponsors. If you would like to rescind this permission, please contact us directly.

name: Tech Unity Cookout
description:
How pleasant it is when the people of tech come together in unity!

After a year of talking about it, we’re finally doing it: a chill summer cookout for everyone in Baltimore tech. No booths, no buzzwords — just food, sun, and a mic for group leaders to explain their mission.

We are hoping to cultivate a virtuous environment of Baltimore's best tech leaders, to share organizational tips, and to lean into Smalltimore's unique social strengths in building the future economy. Ultimately, we hope to foster the relationships that create better lives for ourselves and our people.

Participating groups

* **Code Collective**
* **Baltimore Node Makerspace**
* **Baltimore Tech**
* **Brady UX**
* **DevOps Columbia**
* **Blk Tech Connect**
* **Baltimore Indie Game Devs**

Free admission. Food will be provided!

The event will occur at Wonderground Park in collaboration with the Greenmount West Community Association. It's nearby Guilford Hall Brewery, home of EquiTech Tuesday and occasional host of Baltimore Rhythm Festival.

In the case of inclement weather, the event will occur at Mount Vernon Marketplace, or at a local bar.

name: IT Social Baltimore | Data, Technology, Cybersecurity, IT
description: The FIRST regular after-work social event. Friends, Music, Drinks, Games, Prizes!

name: MD Cyber & IT Recruitment Event
description:
Veterans, transitioning service members, military spouses, federal-impacted workers, and other job seekers are invited to connect with 50 employers hiring in cyber, IT support, and networking. Register by June 23 at https://lnkd.in/g6NBW58r. 
Employers interested in participating can showcase their open positions and connect with top-tier talent. To reserve your space, contact Mark Pindell at mark.pindell@maryland.gov.

location.name: Crown Plaza Hotel
location.address: 173 Jennifer Road Annapolis, Maryland 21401 United States

name: BLK Tech Connect #3: Black Tech Brunch
description: We’re taking over Flock — one of Baltimore’s hottest modern restaurants — for an curated brunch where innovation & community come together.

name: From Code to Cure - A Biotech Networking Event
description: Start your morning with innovation, insight, & meaningful connections. Join us for Montgomery College’s signature biotech networking event.

name: The Art of Advocacy
description: Stand Up was established on May 11, 2019 by twenty students who were inspired to continue to fight against gun violence after raising awareness about its negative effects for a school project. We even decided to name Stand Up after the poem created for the presentations. Since we established Stand Up, we have accomplished a lot, and we hope to progress further in our change-making journey with your help!

name: Maker Faire Downtown Columbia
description: Maker Faire is a gathering of fascinating, curious people who enjoy learning and who love sharing what they can do. From engineers to artists to scientists to crafters, Maker Faire is a venue for these "makers" to show hobbies, experiments, projects. We call it the Greatest Show (& Tell) on Earth - a family-friendly showcase of invention, creativity, and resourcefulness. Glimpse the future and get inspired!

name: AGILE: Building Earth’s Future From Space
description: Space is no longer just the final frontier—it’s becoming part of the infrastructure that powers our everyday lives. From emergency communications and real-time logistics to breakthroughs in biotech and materials science, space-based technologies are moving from science fiction to daily function. This session of the AGILE Global Innovation Series explores the accelerating role of space

name: 2025 Inside Series Featuring Coty
description: Whether you’re passionate about the latest trends, eager to hear from industry leaders, or just looking for a unique networking opportunity, this is the perfect event for you. Join us for an exciting journey where creativity, beauty, and business collide — and walk away with fresh insights, connections, and inspiration to fuel your own success.

name: County Executive Ball's 5th Annual STEM Awards
description: The County Executive is looking forward to honoring our students who competed in various STEM competitions this school year.

name: Tential Tech Tuesday
description: Join us for Tential Tech Tuesday, where we dive into all things tech-related in a fun and interactive way!
